title: FAIR Founded
description: Facebook officially established the Facebook AI Research (FAIR) laboratory, with Yann LeCun serving as Chief Scientist, aiming to promote open collaboration in AI research.

title: Acquisition of Wit.ai
description: Facebook acquired the voice processing startup Wit.ai to strengthen the conversational AI capabilities of the Messenger platform, laying the foundation for subsequent AI assistant development.

title: FAIR Paris Lab Established
description: Facebook established the FAIR Europe laboratory in Paris, led by renowned French computer vision expert Yann LeCun, expanding Europe's AI research capacity.

title: PyTorch Officially Released
description: Facebook AI Research released the open-source deep learning framework PyTorch, optimized for Python with dynamic computation graph support, later becoming one of the most popular AI R&D frameworks.

title: ParlAI Conversational AI Platform Released
description: Facebook released the ParlAI open-source platform for training and testing conversational AI models, integrating multiple standard dialogue datasets.

title: PyTorch 1.0 Released
description: Facebook released PyTorch 1.0, integrating Caffe2 and ONNX capabilities, providing production deployment capabilities and accelerating industrial adoption.

title: Acquisition of CTRL-labs Brain-Computer Interface Startup
description: Facebook acquired CTRL-labs, a startup developing brainwave-controlled computer interfaces, for $0.5–1 billion, laying the foundation for AR/VR interaction.

title: Facebook Officially Renamed Meta
description: Mark Zuckerberg announced that the company was officially renamed Meta Platforms, shifting focus to the Metaverse and AI R&D, with resources being significantly reallocated to the AI field.

title: LLaMA Large Language Model Released
description: Meta released LLaMA (Large Language Model Meta AI), offering multiple sizes from 7B to 65B, limited to research use, but gaining widespread attention due to its outstanding performance.

title: Make-A-Video Video Generation Model Released
description: Meta launched Make-A-Video, capable of automatically generating short video clips from text descriptions, one of the most advanced text-to-video generation technologies at the time.

title: LLaMA Model Weights Leaked
description: LLaMA model weights leaked through sites like 4chan. Although unauthorized, the widespread open-source adoption drove the development of the open-source large language model ecosystem.

title: LLaMA 2 Released with Commercial Open Licensing
description: Meta released LLaMA 2, offering versions from 7B to 70B parameters, with licenses available for commercial adoption, becoming an important milestone for open-source large language models.

title: Ray-Ban Meta Smart Glasses Released
description: Meta collaborated with EssilorLuxottica to launch the Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses, featuring the Meta AI assistant, camera, and music functions, an important attempt at consumer-grade AI wearable devices.

title: Code Llama Code Model Released
description: Meta released Code Llama, a code generation model based on LLaMA 2, supporting multiple programming languages and offering free commercial licensing.

title: Llama 3 Released
description: Meta released the Llama 3 series, including 8B and 70B specifications, achieving best-in-class performance among models of the same size, further consolidating its leadership position in open-source models.

title: Llama 3.1 Released, 405B Parameters Debut
description: Meta launched Llama 3.1, including the 405B parameter version, the largest open-source model at the time, with performance competitive against closed-source commercial models, continuing to drive the open-source AI landscape.

title: Llama 3.2 Multimodal Version Released
description: Meta released Llama 3.2, supporting visual understanding, including lightweight 1B and 3B mobile models, expanding to edge devices such as smart glasses.

title: Llama 3.3 Released
description: Meta released Llama 3.3 70B, significantly improving performance while maintaining the same parameter scale, demonstrating the ability to continuously optimize model efficiency.

title: Llama 4 Released
description: Meta released the Llama 4 series, adopting a hybrid architecture design, supporting multimodal understanding and longer context, pushing open-source model capabilities to new heights.
